==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[649]] and [[664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Tokyo Imperial University Archives, ''[[Dai Nihon Komonjo]]'', Vol. 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. * Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. ISBN 4-642-07492-9. == See Also == * {{ill|History of the court Rank and title system|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of the court Rank and title system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a {{ill|Rank within the cap Rank system|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank within the cap Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}} used in Japan from {{ill|649|ja|649th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/649|qq=}} to {{ill|685|ja|685th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/685|qq=}}. The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after {{ill|664|ja|664th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/664|qq=}}, making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika|qq=}} 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of {{ill|Kaijo District|ja|海上郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijo District|qq=}} in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}}''': Recognized in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi|qq=}} 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Transition of the Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of the Cap and Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>

Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.

The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of {{ill|Kaijo District|ja|海上郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijo District|qq=}} in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}}''': Recognized in {{ill|Hakuchi|ja|白雉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchi|qq=}} 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.

[[sn:Koetsuga]] [[ja:小乙下]] [[en:Koetsuga]]
==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[649]] and [[664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Tokyo Imperial University Archives, ''[[Dai Nihon Komonjo]]'', Vol. 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. * Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a {{ill|Rank within the cap Rank system|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank within the cap Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}} used in Japan from [[:en:649|649]] to [[:en:685|685]]. The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after [[:en:664|664]], making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of [[:en:Kaijō District, Chiba|Kaijo District]] in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}}''': Recognized in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.

The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after [[:en:664|664]], making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of [[:en:Kaijō District, Chiba|Kaijo District]] in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}}''': Recognized in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.

[[sn:Koetsuga]] [[ja:小乙下]] [[en:Koetsuga]]
==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[649]] and [[664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Tokyo Imperial University Archives, ''[[Dai Nihon Komonjo]]'', Vol. 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. * Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. ISBN 4-642-07492-9. == See Also == * {{ill|History of the court Rank and title system|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of the court Rank and title system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.

revision.text:
'''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a {{ill|Rank within the cap Rank system|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank within the cap Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}} used in Japan from [[:en:649|649]] to [[:en:685|685]]. The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after [[:en:664|664]], making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of [[:en:Kaijō District, Chiba|Kaijo District]] in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}}''': Recognized in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Transition of the Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of the Cap and Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

[[sn:Koetsuga]] [[ja:小乙下]]
==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[649]] and [[664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Tokyo Imperial University Archives, ''[[Dai Nihon Komonjo]]'', Vol. 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. * Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. ISBN 4-642-07492-9. == See Also == * {{ill|History of the court Rank and title system|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of the court Rank and title system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>

revision.text:
'''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a {{ill|Rank within the cap Rank system|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank within the cap Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} used in Japan from [[:en:649|649]] to [[:en:685|685]]. The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after [[:en:664|664]], making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of [[:en:Kaijō District, Chiba|Kaijo District]] in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}}''': Recognized in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.

[[sn:Koetsuga]] [[ja:小乙下]]
==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[649]] and [[664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Tokyo Imperial University Archives, ''[[Dai Nihon Komonjo]]'', Vol. 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. * Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. ISBN 4-642-07492-9. == See Also == * {{ill|History of the court Rank and title system|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of the court Rank and title system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[:en:649|649]] and [[664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}

"Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. revision.text:
'''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a {{ill|Rank within the cap Rank system|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank within the cap Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} used in Japan from [[:en:649|649]] to [[:en:685|685]]. The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after [[:en:664|664]], making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of [[:en:Kaijō District, Chiba|Kaijo District]] in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}}''': Recognized in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.

[[sn:Koetsuga]] [[ja:小乙下]]
==Merged second translation== '''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a [[Rank]] in the Japanese [[court Rank system]] used between [[:en:649|649]] and [[685]]. The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[:en:664|664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[:en:649|649]] and [[:en:664|664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>

The Rank's position changed with time: it was the second lowest Rank until [[:en:664|664]], and after that, it became the third lowest. It was below [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Risshin|Risshin]] or [[Daiken|Daiken]] depending on the period.
== Overview ==
Shōotsuge was introduced in [[Taika]] 5 (649 CE) as part of the [[nineteen-Rank system]].<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Taika 5 (649 CE), 2nd month. Unless otherwise noted, references to events in the ''Nihon Shoki'' follow the dates provided therein.</ref> It was created by splitting the previous [[Seven Colors and Thirteen Ranks|Shikishijūsankai]] Rank of [[Kokuro|Kokuro]] into [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]] and Shōotsuge. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, with [[Risshin|Risshin]] being the lowest.
In the 3rd year of [[Emperor Tenji]] (664 CE), the [[twenty-six-Rank system]] was implemented, dividing Shōotsu into three Ranks: [[Shōotsujō|Shōotsujō]], [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]], and Shōotsuge. Under this system, Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, below [[Shōotsuchū|Shōotsuchū]] and above [[Daiken|Daiken]].
In the 14th year of [[Emperor Tenmu]] (685 CE), the [[forty-eight-Rank system]] was introduced, and the naming conventions for Ranks were overhauled, resulting in the abolition of Shōotsuge.
== Individuals Awarded Shōotsuge ==
Three records in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' mention individuals granted this Rank. [[Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi]] was an envoy to [[Tang China]] in [[Hakuchi]] 5 (654 CE). [[Saniguna]] and [[Aobiru]] were [[Emishi]] chieftains who surrendered to [[Abe no Hirafu]]'s northern expedition in [[Saimei]] 4 (658 CE) and were brought to the capital, entertained, and granted court Ranks. The [[Fort Lords]] of [[Tukisara Castle|Tsukisara Fort]] and [[Numusari Castle|Numusari Fort]] were also assigned this Rank. [[Kamimitu]] served as a junior envoy on a mission to the [[Tanegashima|island of Tane]] in [[Tenmu]] 8 (679 CE), under [[Yamabaikainomuraji|Yamabaikainomuraji]] of [[Ōotsuge]] Rank.
The ''[[Shōsōin documents]]'' mention a Shōotsuge named [[Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|Oshikami]], whose grandfather served as a minor official in [[Shimousa Province]] during [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign.<ref>''Dai Nihon Komonjo,'' Vol. 3, pp. 149–150.</ref> Another record in the ''[[Hitachi Fudoki]]'' describes [[Mibu no Maro]], an official from [[Ibaraki Province]], who worked with [[Naka Province Chief|Naka Province Chief]] [[Mibu no Fukiko]] to establish [[Namegata District]] in [[Hakuchi]] 4 (653 CE).<ref>''Hitachi Fudoki,'' Namegata District section.</ref>
=== Individuals Known to Hold Shōotsuge === * {{ill|Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Othera no Hihohe no Oshikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - During [[Emperor Kōtoku]]'s reign (645–654 CE), a minor official in [[Kaijo District]]. * {{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 4 (653 CE) as [[Ibaraki Province Chief]]. * {{ill|Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hataru no Oi|qq=}} - Recorded in Hakuchi 5 (654 CE), served as a Tang China envoy. * {{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as chief of [[Numusari Fort]]. * {{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), served as minor official of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Fort Lord|ja|柵造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fort Lord|qq=}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Tsukisara Castle]]. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Inatsumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Promoted on Saimei 4 (658 CE), chief of [[Numusari Castle]]. * {{ill|Kamimitu|ja|上光父|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimitu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Recorded in Tenmu 8 (679 CE) as junior envoy to Tanegashima.
== Wooden Tablets with Shōotsuge Markings ==
Two wooden tablets from the [[Asuka Palace site]] bear inscriptions of the Rank Shōotsuge. One, discovered during the 51st excavation in 1975, is labeled "Shōotsuge Rank" alongside tablets inscribed with [[Shōsanjō|Shōsanjō]] and [[Ōhanage|Ōhanage]]. Based on the timeframe of the latter Rank, the tablet dates between [[:en:649|649]] and [[:en:664|664]].<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," pp. 100–102.</ref>
The second, found during the 104th excavation in 1984, was inscribed on shavings along with "Ōotsuge," "Year of Xin-Si," and "Intercalary Month." The year Xin-Si corresponds to [[Tenmu]] 10 (681 CE), which also had an intercalary month.<ref>Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," p. 104.</ref>
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == * Tokyo Imperial University Archives, ''[[Dai Nihon Komonjo]]'', Vol. 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. * Nitō, Atsushi. "Capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara," in Kunio Hirano and Yasumitsu Suzuki, eds., ''Chronicles Told by Wooden Tablets'' Vol. 1 (Capitals and Daily Life), Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996. ISBN 4-642-07492-9. == See Also == * {{ill|History of the court Rank and title system|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of the court Rank and title system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Shōotsuge''' (小乙下) was a {{ill|Rank within the cap Rank system|ja|冠位|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|lt=Crown Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank within the cap Rank system|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 冠位・位階制度の変遷}} used in Japan from [[:en:649|649]] to [[:en:685|685]]. The Rank's position within the hierarchy changed after [[:en:664|664]], making it either the second or third lowest Rank. Initially positioned below {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}, it later Ranked below {{ill|Shōotsuchū|ja|小乙中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsuchū|qq=}} and above {{ill|Daiken|ja|大建|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiken|qq=}}.
== Overview == The Rank was introduced in [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5 (649) as part of the {{ill|19 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位十九階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/19 Cap Rank System|qq=}}, splitting the former {{ill|Shōkoku|ja|小黒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkoku|qq=}} Rank into {{ill|Shōotsujō|ja|小乙上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōotsujō|qq=}} and Shōotsuge<ref>『日本書紀』大化5th year （649th year ）2月条。</ref>. Shōotsuge was the 18th Rank out of 19, positioned just above {{ill|Risshin|ja|立身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Risshin|qq=}}.
In the 3rd year of Emperor Tenji's reign (664), the {{ill|26 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位二十六階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26 Cap Rank System|qq=}} was established, subdividing Shōotsu into three levels: upper, middle, and lower. Shōotsuge became the 24th Rank out of 26, just above Daiken.
The Rank was abolished in the 14th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685) with the introduction of the {{ill|48 Cap Rank System|ja|冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48 Cap Rank System|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Individuals Holding the Rank == The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] mentions several individuals who held the Shōotsuge Rank: * '''{{ill|Tajihi no Hinamori|ja|他田日奉部忍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Hinamori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Served as a minor official (Shōryō) of [[:en:Kaijō District, Chiba|Kaijo District]] in Shimōsa Province during Emperor Kōtoku's reign. * '''{{ill|Mibu no Maro|ja|壬生麿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu no Maro|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 壬生麻呂}}''': Recognized in [[:en:Hakuchi (era)|Hakuchi]] 4 (653) as Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko, involved in founding Namekata District. * '''{{ill|Nakatomi no Hitooshio|ja|中臣間人老|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Hitooshio|qq=}}''': Mentioned in Hakuchi 5 (654) as part of a Tang envoy delegation. * '''{{ill|Saniguna|ja|沙尼具那|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saniguna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': An Emishi chieftain who surrendered in Saimei 4 (658) and became a district official. * '''{{ill|Aobiru|ja|青蒜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aobiru|qq=}}''': Another Emishi leader from Saimei 4 (658), later appointed as a minor official in Tsugaru. - An unidentified individual in Saimei 4 (658), tasked with constructing the Tsugisara Palisade. * '''{{ill|Ōtomo no Inazumi|ja|大伴稲積|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Inazumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Recognized for constructing the Nunozashi Palisade in Saimei 4 (658). * '''{{ill|Ue no Mitsuchichi|ja|上光父|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ue no Mitsuchichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''': Assigned to Tanegashima as a minor envoy in Tenmu 8 (679).
== Shōotsuge in Mokkan (Wooden Tablets) == Two mokkan (wooden tablets) mentioning the Rank Shōotsuge have been excavated from the {{ill|Asuka Palace Site|ja|飛鳥京跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Palace Site|qq=}}: - One found in the 51st excavation (1975) contains the phrase "Shōotsuge Rank" and is dated to 649–664 based on associated Ranks. - Another found in the 104th excavation (1984) includes the term "Shōotsuge" along with references to "Daiken" and the year "辛巳" (681).
== Notes == {{reflist}}
== References == - Tokyo Imperial University Historical Compilation Department, 『大日本古文書』 Volume 3, Tokyo Imperial University, 1902. - Nito Atsushi, "Asuka and Fujiwara Capitals," in {{ill|The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|ja|木簡が語る古代史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Ancient History Told by Wooden Tablets|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1996, ISBN 4-642-07492-9.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Transition of the Cap and Rank System|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of the Cap and Rank System|13=User:Immanuelle/Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|qq=}}
